gradeLabel: Open weights under a permissive license

ladderVerdict.justification: The Apache 2.0 license permits commercial use, modification, and redistribution, requiring only preservation of license and notice, which satisfies the L2 openness rung.

license.name: Apache 2.0 (open-weight releases)
notAllowed: Use the Mistral trademarks without permission
license.commercialNote: Mistral open-weight releases are under Apache 2.0, which permits commercial use, modification and redistribution with attribution.

deployment.throughput: ~25-30 tokens/s on GTX 1070 (8GB VRAM) with Q6_K quantization
deployment.verdict: runs on a single consumer GPU

technical.parameters: Mistral Large 3: ~100B+ (per model card); 7B / 8B open-weight also available
technical.quantizationImpact: BF16 7B ~14 GB VRAM; Q4_K_M GGUF ~5 GB VRAM (≈65% reduction)
technical.tokensPerSec: ~25-30 tokens/s on GTX 1070 (8GB VRAM) with Q6_K quantization

private.text: Apache 2.0 license allows commercial use, modification, and redistribution; the 7B variant runs on consumer hardware with as little as 5 GB VRAM when quantized.

enterprise.text: No usage‑based thresholds or royalties apply; enterprises can deploy the model commercially under Apache 2.0 with only attribution required.

nonprofit.text: Same permissive terms apply; nonprofits can freely use, modify, and redistribute the model under Apache 2.0.

disagreements: Source [5] reports 6 GB VRAM for Mistral 7B (likely FP16) whereas authoritative facts list 5 GB VRAM for Q4_K_M quantization; source [4] mentions 16‑32 GB RAM or ≥16 GB VRAM GPU for Mistral Small 4 Q4/Q5, which pertains to a different model and not the Mistral 7B/Large 3 family.

sources.title: Hardware specs for GGUF 7B/13B/30B parameter models #3847
